messages.text: What custom tool would you like to build?
create a kanban board to track my work

**Defining The Kanban Board**

I'm now zeroing in on defining the core Kanban board. I'm focusing on the strictest scope discipline, creating only what's explicitly requested: tracking work. I'm prioritizing radical simplicity and component architecture as I structure the approach. The goal is a focused, functional tracking tool, nothing more.


**Focusing On Design & Layout**

I'm now zeroing in on the UI design. I'm prioritizing radical simplicity and adherence to the M3 design language, using a white background, a light container, a primary accent, and a neutral outline. I'm avoiding gradients, in-app headers, logos, and badges, or unnecessary embellishments. I've also verified that the core board structure will be a 3-column Kanban layout: "To Do", "In Progress", and "Done". I have a short list of team members to start populating the board.


**Implementing Real-time Sync**

I'm now integrating real-time multiplayer synchronization. I'm focusing on the flat-file state synchronization API. I plan to use short polling every two seconds, hitting endpoints for saving and loading state data. I'll get the environment ID from `window.__sandboxEnvId`. I've also implemented the tool naming mandate and ensured a clear title tag is used. I am avoiding redundant in-app headers, logos, and icons and starting directly with the core application interface.


**Prioritizing Direct Outputs**

I'm now changing my focus to direct code output. I'm prioritizing immediate markdown code blocks and file focus, particularly on `index.html`. I'm avoiding local file system interactions entirely. I've re-read Rule 5, which clarifies that direct output is preferred, ensuring efficiency and speed. I'm skipping any redundant interfaces.


**Designing The Output**

I'm now fully focused on directly outputting code. I'm prioritizing `index.html` structure and design, incorporating Tailwind CSS via CDN. I'm focusing on the Material Design 3 and Polaris UI layout. I've also incorporated the correct colors, and fonts, and also the `index.html` layout structure, and now including all columns. I'm also planning the core features. I am implementing both drag & drop, and small control buttons for maximum usability, or clean move arrows/select dropdown.


**Adding Task Interaction Methods**

I'm now implementing both drag-and-drop and move buttons or a dropdown for task movement. The HTML5 `draggable="true"` attribute provides robust drag-and-drop with simple JS. For task interaction, I'll add edit and delete actions. I'm also planning to implement multi-user live sync, using `window.__sandboxEnvId` for state synchronization or localStorage as a fallback. I'll provide a list of assignees with real team members.


**Defining Core Elements**

I'm now focusing on defining core elements. I'm prioritizing inline structure within `index.html` for everything. I'm concentrating on building a beautiful, interactive, and minimal board, eliminating all redundant headers, logos, and in-app elements. The goal is to start directly with the core application interface. I'm integrating the list of users provided to the board.


**Structuring The Interface**

I'm now prioritizing the structure of the main interface components. I am planning a control row with an "Add Task" button (M3 primary style) and a search/filter input, possibly a team member filter. Below the control row, I'll layout three columns ("To Do", "In Progress", "Done"). Each column will be a card with a surface-container background, neat border radius, and an outline border. Inside each column will be task cards with flat white backgrounds, rounded corners, a muted outline, Google Sans Text at 14px, an assignee with an avatar image, and task controls. I've re-reviewed the synchronization logic.


**Populating The Board**

I'm now populating the board with initial tasks. I've integrated task data directly into the `index.html` structure, including titles, descriptions, statuses, and assignees, like Emily Thomas and Wurtz. I'm prioritizing data-driven interface updates. Each task now has an ID for proper lifecycle management.


**Integrating Team Avatars**

I'm now integrating team avatars into task cards, ensuring a visual representation of assignees. I'm focusing on pulling avatar URLs from the provided user data and associating them with tasks. I'm testing the avatar display and layout for the team members to ensure the visual elements integrate with the overall UI. I've re-reviewed the synchronization logic.


**Refining Drag & Drop**

I'm now refining the drag-and-drop interactions to guarantee smooth operation. I've focused on ensuring the `setData` method stores the dragged task ID properly on drag start. I am planning to remove the `ondragover` function because the browser's default behavior suits the purpose. My current focus is to refine the implementation for the current state in memory.


**Implementing State Synchronization**

I'm now implementing the state synchronization logic to prevent flashing during updates, focusing on three strategies: tracking active editing, checking state changes using `JSON.stringify`, and a standard client poll. I will implement a check of localStorage if `envId` is absent. Also, I'm integrating logic to load synced state and avoid unnecessary overwrites.


**Implementing State Saving**

I'm now integrating the state saving and loading mechanisms with robust error handling, and I'm ensuring graceful fallback to local storage when the sandbox environment ID is unavailable. I'm focusing on minimizing UI flicker during synchronization by tracking active edits and comparing state changes. I'm also including all fonts as requested: Google Sans Text, with appropriate font weights and also Google Sans.


**Revising Color Scheme**

I'm now revising the background and container colors to adhere to M3 guidelines. I'm focusing on the `surface` and `surface-container` values to ensure a clean visual hierarchy. The page body will use `#ffffff`, with a clean white color. The Kanban column backgrounds will use `#f0f4f9` for a subtle contrast. This approach creates a clean interface.


**Designing The Controls**

I'm now designing the control row for the Kanban board. I've added an "Add Task" button with the M3 primary style, using `#0b57d0` background and `#ffffff` text, rounded. A search input with a muted outline is also in place, along with quick filter buttons. I am now adding a clean team member dropdown for filtering tasks. I am focused on the "Add Task" modal, prioritizing a clean, minimalist design with a title and description input. I'm choosing a standard select for assigning the assignee, displaying "Name (Email)".


**Adding Task Interaction**

I'm now integrating the custom modal components for adding new tasks, including title, description, and status selection. I've incorporated a save and cancel button and validated the provided team member list for assignee dropdown. I'm focusing on clean, concise input fields, using an M3 design. My next steps are populating sample tasks using provided team members with realistic titles and correct assignments. I'll focus on a few realistic tasks, like "Design homepage header components" to Stephanie Goodson.


**Adding Initial Tasks**

I'm now adding default tasks and refining the interface's style, incorporating Tailwind classes while adhering to the Material Design 3 theme, including new tasks for review and refactoring. I've focused on customizing colors with the primary, on-primary, primary-container, surface, surface-container, and outline tokens, using Tailwind's classes for precise control. I'm also ensuring the HTML loads Google Sans, Tailwind CSS, and standard inline SVG icons.


**Adding SVG Icons**

I'm now integrating inline SVG icons for actions, using extremely clean code for "add", "edit", "delete", "user", "move left", and "move right" functions. I've re-read Rule 7, confirming I must output directly to `index.html`. Therefore, the following markdown block contains the complete content of the updated file.


**Defining CSS Variables**

I'm now defining the Tailwind CSS variables and initial styles. I'm focusing on adhering to the M3 theme for all colors and ensuring proper Google Sans font integration. I've confirmed I don't need any write-file tools to complete.

I've provided the code directly for the updated application.

To Do

Design homepage header components

**Refining HTML Structure**

I'm now refining the HTML structure to support core features like task filtering, adding, editing, and deleting. I'm prioritizing accessibility and clean separation of concerns for the board, focusing on the UI with control buttons and task cards. I'm focusing on Tailwind's utility classes to manage UI elements and maintain clean and efficient markup. I'm also preparing to add the core logic for filtering.

**Structuring Core Interactions**

I'm now structuring the core interactions. I'm focusing on the component layout for the Kanban board and the modal's input fields. I'm prioritizing clean and efficient markup for filtering, and adding task functionalities. I'm also preparing the application state to manage task editing. The goal is to provide a clean and interactive task management experience.

**Implementing Core Features**

I'm now integrating the core JavaScript logic for task management. I'm focusing on the component layout for the Kanban board, including dynamic addition, editing, and deletion of tasks. I've re-reviewed the team member list. I am prioritizing a clean, efficient, and interactive board with drag and drop, and state synchronization. I'm adding a working state for all active and completed tasks.

**Refining Interaction & Synchronization**

I'm now implementing the complete Javascript interaction logic, including the drag-and-drop feature, the state synchronization, and implementing all the defined event handlers. I'm focusing on refining the core functionality for moving tasks and saving state changes properly. The goal is to provide a smooth, responsive, and synchronized task experience with a custom visual style for the drag and drop features.

**Refining Assignee Picker**

I'm now iterating on the assignee selection method. My current focus is to integrate the radio button for better user experience. I'm focusing on simplicity, visual appeal, and ease of use. I'm considering a scrollable list. I'm evaluating the balance between a grid of avatars and a more straightforward list for 14 team members. I am aiming for a design that visually represents the assigned user.

**Implementing Assignee Picker**

I'm now prioritizing a dropdown menu assignee picker for enhanced usability. I'm focusing on building a custom picker with a button displaying the selected user's avatar and name. The plan includes a search input within the picker for filtering all team members, and a list of matching members. Clicking a team member updates the button and closes the picker. This is all implemented with HTML buttons and lists.

**Refining Dropdown UI**

I'm now refining the assignee dropdown UI for intuitive selection. I'm prioritizing the display of the selected member's avatar and name in the button, with an accessible dropdown list below. The plan involves a search function within the dropdown. I am focusing on a clean design, using HTML buttons and lists for an optimal experience with proper interaction.

**Implementing Default Tasks**

I'm now integrating the provided default tasks with realistic details. I'm focusing on "Refactor workspace layout" and "Implement poll synchronization" tasks. I've re-reviewed the task data, including titles, descriptions, assignees, and statuses, to properly populate the Kanban board with initial data. This involves adding these tasks directly into the HTML structure.

**Refining Data Population**

I'm now integrating the additional tasks with the board, refining the data structure for proper display. I'm focusing on "Review design feedback," "Audit accessibility," and "Conduct database trace analysis." I'm also preparing to add the core logic for filtering by member or status, and incorporating the new team members. I am aiming to ensure a seamless and intuitive task management experience.

**Adding Task Action Logic**

I'm now integrating the core JavaScript functions to handle task interactions, like adding, editing, and deleting tasks. I'm focusing on dynamic updates to the Kanban board and the implemented modal for task actions. I am also preparing to apply these new Javascript functions to all existing tasks.
